#SecretsOfTheElephants is now streaming on @Disney+ and @hulu.

text: These hammerheads are ready for their close-up. #HammerheadSharksUpClose with Bertie Gregory is now streaming on @Disney+ and @hulu, part of #Sharkfest on National Geographic.

authorMeta.name: natgeo
authorMeta.signature: Step into wonder and find your inner explorer with National Geographic 🌎

text: During their historic ten-day, record-breaking flyby of the moon earlier this year, the Artemis II crew lived in incredibly cramped quarters, but this proximity also led to camaraderie. In a never-before-seen clip, recorded on cameras Nat Geo sent along on the voyage, Commander Reid Wiseman and Mission Specialist Jeremy Hansen reflected on the beauty of the moon's surface following their first night of observation, as well as the touching moment when the crew suggested naming a lunar crater after Wiseman's late wife, Carroll.   Discover more insights from life between lunar shifts at the 🔗 in bio.

authorMeta.name: natgeo
authorMeta.signature: Step into wonder and find your inner explorer with National Geographic 🌎

text: Have you ever seen a rotifer? National Geographic Explorer @Ariel Waldman explains how these strange creatures have survived for millions of years—and where to find one.

authorMeta.name: natgeo
authorMeta.signature: Step into wonder and find your inner explorer with National Geographic 🌎

text: A moment of awe. While searching for the Seven Natural Wonders of America, @ABC News’ Deborah Roberts and National Geographic Explorer James Edward Mills captured this beautiful view. Can you guess where they are?   Discover more beauty and nature in the final Wonders of America list, revealed during the 24-hour “Disney Celebrates America” live event special on July 4th.

authorMeta.name: natgeo
authorMeta.signature: Step into wonder and find your inner explorer with National Geographic 🌎

text: A fish has been documented appearing to hold its breath—but it's not the only marine animal to do it. Hammerhead sharks also hold their breath to conserve heat while hunting, and National Geographic Explorer Arzucan Askin says this unique ability could be useful in other ways.

authorMeta.name: natgeo
authorMeta.signature: Step into wonder and find your inner explorer with National Geographic 🌎

text:
After sunset, thousands of stars emerge across the sky above Big Bend National Park. Darkness is hard to measure definitively, but Texas’s largest national park sits within the world’s biggest certified dark sky area—the 15,000-square-mile (38,850 square-kilometer) Greater Big Bend International Dark Sky Reserve. This is one of the finest places in the country to see the Milky Way, offering nighttime visitors a star-spangled sky much as it looked 250 years ago.    

With the 250th anniversary of U.S. independence quickly approaching, we're celebrating the grandest, wildest, and strangest bits of American geography and culture across every state and territory. Discover more Wonders of America at the 🔗 in bio.

authorMeta.name: natgeo
authorMeta.signature: Step into wonder and find your inner explorer with National Geographic 🌎

text: Just checking in on a friend? In meerkat society, a cute nudge like this can go a long way. Beyond their strict social hierarchy, meerkats rely on cooperation to help their clans survive the threats posed by the Kalahari desert—including rival groups. Learn more about the complex social dynamics of meerkats at the 🔗 in bio.

authorMeta.name: natgeo
authorMeta.signature: Step into wonder and find your inner explorer with National Geographic 🌎

text:
To protect her kingdom, Cleopatra needed an army, so she turned to the best that the Roman Empire had to offer. But to win Mark Antony to her side, she put on the performance of a lifetime. 

#CleopatrasFinalSecret is streaming on @Disney+ and @hulu.

authorMeta.name: natgeo
authorMeta.signature: Step into wonder and find your inner explorer with National Geographic 🌎

text:
At the New England Aquarium in Boston, Massachusetts, members of the African penguin colony are living up to two-and-a-half times longer than the 10 to 15 years they reach in the wild. So what happens when they reach an advanced age? They move to a "retirement island" away from the larger colony where they can enjoy a quieter life complete with physical therapy, mental enrichment, and, of course, plenty of fish.    

Experience a day in the life of Boston's geriatric penguins at the 🔗 in bio.
